# KSEB Analytics — Copilot Instructions

## Architecture snapshot

- Laravel 11 backend paired with Inertia + React 18 frontend; Vite drives asset builds and `route()` (Ziggy) wires named PHP routes into TSX components.
- Features stay grouped by domain across layers (e.g. `app/Http/Controllers/Subset`, `app/Models/Subset`, `app/Services/Subset`); mirror this in new code.
- Data ingestion flow: connections → queries → jobs (`app/Services/DataLoader`) with `RunScheduledJob` orchestrating fetch/import via factories and `OperationResult` for error propagation.
- Analytics content is managed through subject areas (`SubjectAreaController`), data details, and subsets/subset groups which power UI dashboards and exports.

## Laravel implementation patterns

- Use Form Requests or Spatie `laravel-data` DTOs for validation; see `app/Http/Requests/**/*` and `CreateDataTable` for how request objects provide typed fields.
- Controllers typically implement `HasMiddleware` to enforce `auth`; return Inertia views with paginated Eloquent data (`SubjectAreaController::index`).
- Business logic lives in dedicated services (e.g. `Subset/SubsetApplyDefaultFilters`, `DataLoader/ImportToDataTable`); prefer injecting services rather than querying models directly in controllers.
- Long-running jobs persist status via `DataLoaderJobStatus`; if you add new job types, make sure to record execution metadata for the scheduler UI.
- Utilities in `app/Libs` (pagination, exception formatting, operation results) are the expected way to standardize responses.

## React + Inertia patterns

- Pages reside in `resources/js/Pages/**` and are auto-resolved by `resolvePageComponent` in `app.tsx`; keep file paths aligned with route names.
- Admin CRUD screens reuse `ListResourcePage` plus the strongly-typed `FormBuilder` and `useCustomForm` hooks—extend these rather than creating bespoke form logic.
- UI primitives come from `resources/js/ui` (shadcn-style components) and feature components live under `resources/js/Components/<Feature>`; split code per feature to stay consistent.
- Axios is globally configured in `bootstrap.ts`; prefer inertia form helpers (`router.get`, `useInertiaPost`) over manual fetch wiring.

## Data & integrations

- Creating a subject area or data detail triggers dynamic table scaffolding via `CreateDataTable`; remember to roll back tables on failures like the existing `store` try/catch.
- Subset documentation is generated by `SubsetDocumentationGenerator`; ensure new subset fields include descriptions so docs aren’t empty.
- Gemini integrations live in `App\Services\GeminiService`; respect `.env` config keys (`app.gemini_*`) and bubble errors through `OperationResult` or structured arrays.
- Mapping and hierarchy lookups use dedicated controllers under `DistributionHierarchy` and `Meta*`; reuse those endpoints from the frontend instead of duplicating queries.

## Tooling & workflows

- Install dependencies with `composer install` and `npm install`; run `npm run dev` for the Vite/Inertia dev server and `php artisan serve` (or Sail) for the API.
- Mandatory checks: `php artisan test` (Pest + `RefreshDatabase`), `vendor/bin/pint` for PHP style, `npm run tsc-check` and ESLint (see `package.json`) for TS quality, `vendor/bin/phpstan analyse` using `phpstan.neon`.
- Seed data/jobs live under `database/seeders` and `database/data`; use factories for tests rather than manual inserts.

## Gotchas & tips

- Keep public APIs authenticated—most routes expect `auth` middleware; follow the resource route conventions in `routes/web.php` when adding endpoints.
- Large JSON imports should stream through the DataLoader fetchers; avoid loading unbounded arrays into memory—copy existing fetcher patterns.
- When adjusting pagination/UI filters, update both the Inertia props and the matching paginator types (`Paginator` interface under `ui/ui_interfaces`).
- Any new backend response consumed by React should return camelCase keys or normalize them in the page component to match existing TS interfaces.
